engagement.justification: Touches the question of why using people as things is wrong via dignity and feelings, but drifts into incoherent claims ('we are okay with being used') and never lands the 'always wrong' thesis cleanly.

coherence.justification: Several self-undermining sentences ('many people would be left alone... and the whole world would seem a better place') and contradictory framing ('we are okay with being used') break the line of reasoning.

substance.justification: Names two real reasons (capacity to feel, social interdependence) but does not push them into a developed argument; the second paragraph collapses into truisms about feelings.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Mostly recoverable UGF, but key conceptual moments are garbled by the restriction ('a kind of love that makes the heart beat fast'), leaving the argument clunky.

engagement.justification: A four-fragment scrap that does not address modus ponens with an indicative conditional or a predicate at all.

coherence.justification: Disconnected fragments ('things that do not change. Your thoughts are strong.') with no logical thread.

substance.justification: No content; pure filler with a dangling question.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Words are UGF but assembled into something near-meaningless; the restriction has not been made to carry any argument.

engagement.justification: Does not engage the asked counterexample to a definition of scientific classification; produces an unrelated dialogue fragment about 'two kinds of group'.

coherence.justification: Fragmented, with stray punctuation and no followed argument.

substance.justification: No counterexample, distinction, or relevant case is offered.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF surface but the meaning collapses; restriction yields no usable argument.

engagement.justification: Directly engages why prior belief shapes perception via the 'glasses' frame and confirmation-seeking, though the final paragraph trails off mid-thought.

coherence.justification: Mostly follows step to step, with a couple of garbled spots ('if the new food makes you believe') and an unfinished close, but the through-line holds.

substance.justification: Develops the glasses analogy and the 'all dogs are mean' example into a real account of motivated perception; stops just short of a sharp non-obvious distinction.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF largely native (glasses, blue tint) with isolated clunk ('new food'); the restriction mostly serves the argument.

engagement.justification: Does not address modus ponens with a material conditional in a deontological frame; two unrelated questions about 'the one who shows the things'.

coherence.justification: Two disconnected sentences, no argument.

substance.justification: No relevant logical or ethical content.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF words but no recoverable argument under them.

engagement.justification: Eventually circles the right topic (why some inferences/deals hold) via the rain-prediction case, but the opening is confused and the central 'deals hold/fall apart' framing is never clearly engaged.

coherence.justification: Long passage with frequent contradictions and broken sentences ('when bad things happens he often thinks the water will stay safe'); hard to follow as a single argument.

substance.justification: Raises a genuine point (does one fact make another happen; correlation vs. cause via clouds/rain) but never resolves or sharpens it.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Recoverable in patches but much of the reasoning is obscured by clumsy UGF phrasing.

engagement.justification: Does not address probabilistic conditional / hypothetical syllogism / modal; a couple of vague rhetorical questions about right and wrong.

coherence.justification: Fragmented, no developed line.

substance.justification: No relevant logical content or distinction.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF surface but argument does not survive the restriction.

engagement.justification: Squarely engages why sharing all you know is not always best, enumerating distinct reasons that bear on the question.

coherence.justification: Clear enumerated structure that mostly follows, with a few garbled clauses ('may wrong thoughts happen', 'If your share everything tells you').

substance.justification: Develops several non-obvious considerations (cognitive overload, misuse, pacing learning, self-protection, fairness) rather than restating; stops short of a single decisive case.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF carries the argument well across the list with only minor clunk.

engagement.justification: Engages the analogy that being good at being a person is like being good at any craft, developing it across learning, imitation, and self-knowledge; cuts off mid-sentence.

coherence.justification: Three-part structure follows, though spots are garbled ('you must already know how to act in a way that is not good') and the ending is truncated.

substance.justification: Pushes the craft/skill analogy with practice, observation, and feedback as developed parallels; the explanatory analogy is genuinely built out.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF mostly native to the practice-skill framing with minor agreement slips ('try to do the same things them do').

engagement.justification: Directly engages what it means for a case to be 'put together well', laying out a clear account of structural integrity.

coherence.justification: Ordered steps largely follow, with a couple of awkward connectives ('The thing after the third thing') and one rough clause about staying with questions.

substance.justification: Identifies non-obvious elements (claim, supporting facts, ordered inference, anticipating objections, conclusion) and how they interlock; thorough though not a single sharp insight.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads naturally for this procedural topic; the restriction does not impede the account.

engagement.justification: Engages 'a case put together well' directly with a coherent recipe for coherence and support.

coherence.justification: Steps mostly chain ('one part... helps the other parts'), with minor awkward ordinal phrasing but no contradictions.

substance.justification: Develops the idea that each fact should follow from the prior and that objections must be handled; solid structure without a striking distinction.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF feels native to the argument-structure topic; clean and recoverable throughout.

engagement.justification: Does not engage the abstraction analogy in mechanical/repair terms; two disjoint fragments about 'taking the side of a'.

coherence.justification: Broken fragments with stray apostrophes; no argument.

substance.justification: No content or analogy developed.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF words but no surviving meaning.

engagement.justification: Gestures at finding hidden parts of a case (look for what does not match, ask more questions) but barely engages and is largely incoherent.

coherence.justification: Garbled syntax ('look for things that do not be the same') with no clear flow.

substance.justification: A faint relevant hint (probe inconsistencies) buried in noise; nothing developed.

coherence.justification: Fragment only; nothing to follow.

substance.justification: No content.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Too sparse for the restriction to carry any argument.

engagement.justification: Engages the evidential point that repetition does not make something true, on the question's own terms, though framed as a generic essay rather than a strict-conditional modus ponens exercise.

coherence.justification: Mostly coherent (sky-is-green example, truth as correspondence), with a few broken lines ('they do not go up and down a path make just by saying').

engagement.justification: Engages how to anticipate another's action before acting, offering a perspective-taking procedure, though it sprawls and loses focus midway.

substance.justification: Develops concrete heuristics (look at past behavior, infer reasons, simulate their view, weigh outcomes) beyond truism, though loosely.

coherence.justification: Disconnected clauses, no argument.

substance.justification: No relevant content.

engagement.justification: Drifts onto a correlation-vs-cause essay (rain examples) rather than the asked counterexample about a compelling argument for God's existence; only tangentially relevant.

expressive_adequacy.justification: Clunky but partly recoverable UGF; several sentences are internally contradictory.

substance.justification: Identifies the relevant move (watch, infer, respond to need) but mostly restates it rather than developing why attentiveness is good in itself.

expressive_adequacy.justification: UGF reads naturally for the topic with little friction.

engagement.justification: Does not engage the 'genuine revolution' counterexample; an obscure dialogue fragment about days and the sun.

engagement.justification: Engages how to decide whose good matters and how much, contrasting aggregate good with per-person right, though briefly.

engagement.justification: Engages how to decide when the right action is unclear, offering a deliberation procedure, despite a confused opening.

coherence.justification: Two readable sentences but they do not connect into an argument about group agreement.

substance.justification: Gestures at deciding by preference vs. facts but develops nothing.

engagement.justification: Drifts into a generic virtue-and-habit essay loosely tied to an argumentative analogy about precedent, but never engages the precedent analogy on its terms; also truncated.

coherence.justification: The path/habit middle is readable, but the later 'person one of the two parts' passage degrades into garbled, partly contradictory text.

substance.justification: Has a real idea (repeated acts shape character, like steps wearing a path) but does not connect it to precedent or push it to a distinction.
